items.headline: Agreement approved for Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. This public art installation adds a new permanent piece to the park's amenities.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council is updating the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan (2015–2040 Community Vision), which guides how the city addresses public health, safety, and emergency preparedness in future development and policy. This update will shape how Cupertino plans for climate resilience, disaster response, and community safety over the next decade.

items.headline: City Council Appoints Permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills a key leadership position responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementation of council policies.

items.headline: City reviews draft Urban Water Master Plan and drought contingency measures
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission is reviewing Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These documents outline how the city will manage water resources and respond to drought conditions.

items.headline: 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grants awarded in spring cycle
items.summary: The city is considering applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program spring cycle on May 18. This program funds local community events and neighborhood projects proposed by residents and organizations.

items.headline: Core Companies permitted for $1.9M office buildout near transit
items.summary: A major office tenant improvement at 333 W Santa Clara St has received its building permit. The downtown location near transit suggests continued commercial development in San José's core.

items.headline: Analog Devices receives permit for major office renovation downtown
items.summary: The tech company has been cleared to build out a $2.25M office tenant improvement at 160 Rio Robles. The significant investment signals continued corporate presence in San José's tech corridor.

items.headline: Council to amend rules on accessory and junior dwelling units
items.summary: City Council will consider updating Palo Alto's zoning code to align with recent state law changes affecting accessory dwelling units (ADUs) and junior ADUs. The ordinance amends multiple chapters of the municipal code and is exempt from environmental review.
